message:
Fix compilation errors: correct Tantivy API types and methods

Critical bug fixes for the search implementation that was broken due to
incorrect Tantivy API usage. The code compiled in earlier commits had
multiple type errors that prevented the project from building.

Root cause analysis:
The previous commits used incorrect type names and method calls based on
assumptions about the Tantivy API that turned out to be wrong for v0.25.0.

Fixes in src/search/query.rs:

1. Fixed document type import
   - Removed: OwnedDocument (does not exist in tantivy::schema)
   - Added: TantivyDocument (the actual document type)
   - Added: Value trait (required for .as_str() method)
   - Removed: OwnedValue (unused after fixing type annotations)

2. Fixed document retrieval type annotation
   - Before: let doc: OwnedDocument = searcher.doc(addr)?;
   - After: let doc = searcher.doc::<TantivyDocument>(addr)?;
   - Uses turbofish syntax to specify the document type parameter

3. Fixed helper function signatures
   - Changed all &OwnedDocument to &TantivyDocument
   - Functions: doc_title(), doc_snippet(), doc_text()

4. Fixed value extraction methods
   - Changed .as_text() to .as_str() (correct method on Value trait)
   - Removed incorrect type annotation |v: &OwnedValue|
   - Let compiler infer closure parameter type (CompactDocValue)

Fixes in src/search/tantivy.rs:

5. Fixed fields_from_schema error handling
   - Changed: .ok_or_else() (wrong - for Option types)
   - To: .map_err() (correct - for Result types)
   - Root cause: schema.get_field() returns Result<Field>, not Option<Field>
   - The Tantivy API returns Result to propagate schema lookup errors

Technical details:

Tantivy v0.25.0 API specifics:
- Document type: TantivyDocument (implements Document trait)
- Field values: CompactDocValue (when reading from stored docs)
- Value trait: Provides .as_str(), .as_i64(), etc. conversion methods
- Schema.get_field(): Returns Result<Field>, not Option<Field>

The Value trait must be in scope to call .as_str() on document values.
This is a common Rust pattern where trait methods require the trait to
be imported even when working with concrete types.

The closure type inference issue arose because CompactDocValue is passed
by value to and_then(), not by reference. Explicit type annotations can
break inference when the expected type differs from the annotated type.

These fixes restore the code to a working state. The project now compiles
cleanly without warnings. All 8 previous commits are preserved in git
history but contained non-compiling code. This commit makes the codebase
functional again.
